prompt: Write a warm greeting to a new colleague in five different languages (your choice). After each, add a one-line note about what's culturally implied by the register you chose.
response:
1. English  
Welcome to the team, Sarah! We’re genuinely thrilled you’re here—don’t hesitate to lean on any of us.  
(Register note: Informal-first names and “lean on us” signal flat hierarchies typical of U.S. tech and start-up culture.)

2. Japanese  
山田さん、初めまして。チームに加わってくださり、心よりお迎え申し上げます。今後ともご指導・ご鞭撻のほどよろしくお願い申し上げます。  
(Register note: The humble keigo “お迎え申し上げます” and the bowing verb “よろしく” preserve vertical respect, cushioning the newcomer’s entry.)

3. Spanish  
¡Hola Ana! Qué alegría que estés con nosotros; ya eres una más de la familia. Si necesitas algo, aquí estoy.  
(Register note: Instant “tú” and “familia” create warm confianza, showing that Latin-workplaces collapse distance quickly.)

4. German  
Schön, dass Sie jetzt zu uns stoßen, Herr Dr. Weber. Ich freue mich auf die Zusammenarbeit und stehe Ihnen jederzeit zur Verfügung.  
(Register note: Formal “Sie” plus title “Herr Dr.” mirrors German insistence on professional decorum until mutual consent to switch.)

5. Arabic  
أهلاً وسهلاً بك بيننا، يا أستاذ كريم. نورت الفريق، وإن شاء الله نكون عند حسن ظنك.  
(Register note: The religious tag “إن شاء الله” and praise “نورت” weave faith & courtesy together, hallmarks of Levantine workplace warmth.)
